zh.lifeSummary: Matilda de Bray於大約1218年出生在英國波藍德洛可利福。她於大約1240年與William de Hardreshull在英國林可爾史爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1291年10月23日享年74歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Matilda de Bray于大约1218年出生在英国波藍德洛可利福。她于大约1240年与William de Hardreshull在英国林可爾史爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。她去世于1291年10月23日享年74岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bray
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the places in Berkshire and Devon. The former is probably named with Old French bray ‘marsh’, the latter from the Cornish element bre ‘hill’.
English: perhaps a topographic name from northern Middle English bra ‘steep (river) bank’ or ‘brow of a hill’, denoting someone who lived at such a place.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in Normandy or Picardy called Bray (Eure, Calvados, Aisne, Somme); see 6.

variants.name: Dubray
meanings: French: habitational name, with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’, for someone from any of various minor places called Bray (see Bray ).
Altered form of French   Dubreuil  . This surname is common among Native Americans, namely the Cheyenne River Sioux in SD.

variants.name: Braye
meanings: English (Sussex): from the Middle English female personal name Braye. Its origin is unknown but it might derive from Breton bregh ‘fine, brave’.

variants.name: Brais
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Aldea, Andre, Cecile, Fernande, Stephane.

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: Matilda de Bray was born about 1218, in Rockcliffe, Cumberland, England. She married William de Hardreshull about 1240, in Lincolnshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died on 23 October 1291, at the age of 74.

fr.lifeSummary: Matilda de Bray est née environ 1218, à Rockcliffe, Cumberland, Angleterre. Elle a épousé William de Hardreshull environ 1240, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée le 23 octobre 1291, à l’âge de 74 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Matilda de Bray wurde um 1218 in Rockcliffe, Cumberland, England geboren. Sie hat um 1240 in Lincolnshire, England William de Hardreshull geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Sie starb am 23. Oktober 1291 im Alter von 74 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Matilda de Bray è nata il circa 1218 nel Rockcliffe, Cumberland, England. Lei sposato William de Hardreshull circa 1240, nel Lincolnshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì il 23 ottobre 1291, all'età di 74 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Matilda de Bray(은)는 대략 1218년 영국 c벌랜드 록c리프에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1240년 영국 링코실르에서 William de Hardreshull(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1291년 10월 23일 74세의 나이로 사망했다.
